spec_match.rationale: The page now illustrates <usermedia> fallback content, stream/error/cancel events, and getUserMedia fallback instead of a generic capability button proposal.

interactive.rationale: Users can simulate idle, pending, granted, denied, and unsupported states or trigger a real fallback getUserMedia request.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and code sample describe the UserMedia capability element and its progressive-enhancement behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: Denied, pending, unsupported, granted, and idle states are all visible, with recovery guidance for each failure state.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the correct ChromeStatus entry.

design_system.rationale: The simulator uses the shared variables, contrast-safe state panels, and real controls.

summary: The fallback-state simulator now targets <usermedia> specifically and no longer teaches a removed generic capability-button syntax.
